reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: Erdos #993 (unimodality of independence polynomials of trees/forests, Alavi-Erdos-Malde-Schwenk 1987): a broadened structured search finds NO non-unimodal tree or forest. 112,916 generalized 'bush' trees (orders 26-60; 4,445 distinct non-log-concave, log-concavity defect down to -12.2) are all unimodal, and 253,695 forest objects (products/powers up to 20/path-smoothings over the 80 most-severe non-log-concave seeds) are all unimodal. The order-26 non-log-concave trees T_3,4,4 and T*_3,3,4 are reproduced exactly. This extends the verified-unimodal frontier into the forest/product surface that prior tree-only searches (incl. the 2025 PatternBoost sweep to 101 vertices) left untouched. By Hoggar's theorem a forest counterexample requires a non-log-concave component, so the non-log-concave families are the only place one can live.
attempt.detail: Frozen exact verifier: tree-DP independence polynomial (in/out DP, big-int), self-tested 436/436 trees + 6/6 forests vs brute force. Seeds: families T_3,m,n / T*_3,m,n generalized to parametric bushes (root degree 2-5, per-branch child count 2-6, pendant depths 1-3). Forest surface justified by Hoggar (convolution preserves log-concavity => counterexample needs a non-log-concave component, smallest at order 26). Scripts: verify_993_kernel.py, search_993.py, search_993_v2.py, verify_993_result.py (registered).
attempt.reusable_for: exact independence-polynomial kernel + unimodality/log-concavity scanners; non-log-concave tree seed generator; forest-product unimodality surface

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
